skill.name: mobile-debugging
skill.description: Debug React Native and Expo apps with guided checks for Metro, native modules, simulators, logs, crashes, builds, and performance issues.
skill.summary: Troubleshoot React Native and Expo application failures with focused mobile debugging workflows.
skill.author: BabakBar

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer found many shell-command patterns because this skill is a React Native debugging guide with Bash examples. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, malicious network access, or executable bundled script was found, but several examples delete caches, reset devices, install tools, or kill processes.

medium_findings.title: Destructive Debugging Commands Require Confirmation
medium_findings.description: The guide includes commands that remove dependencies and temporary Metro or Haste caches, reset simulators, wipe emulator state, and kill a process on port 8081. These are legitimate mobile debugging steps, but they can delete local project state or interrupt a developer process if run without confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines directly show destructive or forceful local operations. The surrounding context is normal debugging guidance, so the concern is operational risk rather than malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Broad Bash Access for Local Debugging
medium_findings.description: The skill declares Bash access and provides many local diagnostic commands for package managers, native build tools, device tools, and log collection. This is expected for React Native debugging, but marketplace users should understand that the skill may ask to run commands that affect the local project and connected devices.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tool list and command blocks are explicit. The commands are common debugging commands, but their breadth creates a real permission and side-effect concern.

low_findings.title: Static Root Deletion Alert Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported recursive deletion on root or home, but the cited lines target temporary Metro and Haste cache paths under /tmp. This is not evidence of root or home directory deletion, although wildcard deletion still deserves care.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact cited commands use /tmp cache prefixes, not root or home paths. The finding is therefore a scanner overmatch.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Crypto Alert Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported a weak cryptographic algorithm at the description line, but that line only describes crash analysis for React Native debugging. No cryptographic function or hash algorithm is present at that location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is plain metadata text and contains no crypto API or algorithm call. This is a clear false positive.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to public documentation for React Native, Expo, and Flipper. No code sends data to these URLs, and no evidence of data exfiltration was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only in a Resources section. They are user-facing documentation links, not network request code.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Are Debugging Commands
low_findings.description: The device and version inspection commands list logs, connected devices, and package versions. These commands support mobile debugging and do not show covert collection, persistence, or exfiltration behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly scoped to React Native, Expo, iOS simulator, Android emulator, and dependency checks. No evidence shows collection beyond local troubleshooting context.

dangerous_patterns.title: Forceful Shell Operations in Debugging Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The command examples include recursive deletion, simulator reset, emulator termination, and kill -9 command substitution. These patterns should require explicit user confirmation before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The risky shell operations are present as written. They are not malicious by themselves, but they can cause unintended local data or process loss.

content.value_statement: React Native issues often span JavaScript, native builds, devices, and Metro state. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured workflow for finding likely causes.
actual_capabilities: Guides troubleshooting for Metro bundler cache and module resolution problems.
Suggests native reset workflows for iOS pods, Android Gradle builds, and Expo prebuilds.
Points to simulator, emulator, and device log commands for crash investigation.
Provides React Native performance checks for slow renders and cleanup-related memory leaks.
Organizes debugging around reproduction, recent changes, errors, dependencies, platform, environment, and logs.
limitations: It is a guidance skill, not an automated crash analyzer.
Some commands can delete caches, reset devices, or kill local processes.
It does not replace platform-specific tools such as Xcode, Android Studio, or Flipper.
It requires accurate project context and error messages from the user.

use_cases.title: Resolve Metro Startup Failures
use_cases.description: Use the checklist to clear likely cache and dependency causes before deeper investigation.

use_cases.title: Investigate Native Build Errors
use_cases.description: Follow platform-specific checks for pods, Gradle, Expo prebuilds, and native module compatibility.

use_cases.title: Triage Crashes and Slow Screens
use_cases.description: Collect logs, isolate recent changes, and inspect render or cleanup problems.

prompt_templates.title: Start Basic Debugging
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner triage for a new React Native issue.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me debug this React Native issue. Ask for the minimum details first, then guide me through safe checks before suggesting destructive commands.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Metro Problems
prompt_templates.scenario: Metro bundler fails, hangs, or cannot resolve modules.
prompt_templates.prompt: Diagnose my Metro bundler issue. Use the error message and recent changes to decide which cache, dependency, or import checks to try.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Native Errors
prompt_templates.scenario: iOS, Android, or Expo native build failures.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this native mobile build error and create a platform-specific debugging plan for iOS, Android, or Expo.

prompt_templates.title: Investigate Performance and Crashes
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a React Native investigation plan for this crash or performance issue. Include log sources, reproduction steps, likely causes, and confirmation checks.

output_examples.input: Metro cannot resolve a module after a dependency upgrade.
output_examples.output: A prioritized checklist covering the import path, installed package, lockfile state, Metro cache, and a final dependency reinstall step.

output_examples.input: The Android app crashes on launch with a native module error.
output_examples.output: A platform-specific triage plan that checks Logcat, native module compatibility, Gradle state, and recent dependency changes.

output_examples.input: A screen freezes when navigating from a detail page.
output_examples.output: A debugging path that separates slow render issues, missing cleanup, repeated subscriptions, and platform-specific behavior.

best_practices: Share the exact error message, platform, build type, and recent changes before running commands.
Confirm destructive steps before clearing caches, resetting devices, or killing processes.
Capture logs from Metro, Xcode, Android Studio, or device tools before changing project state.
anti_patterns: Running cache deletion commands before reading the full stack trace.
Resetting all simulators or emulators without confirming saved test data is disposable.
Assuming an issue is JavaScript-only when native logs show the failure.

faq.question: Does this skill run debugging commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and command examples. Users should review and approve each command before execution.

faq.question: Can it help with Expo projects?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes Expo-focused checks for Metro, prebuilds, runtime issues, and common development workflows.

faq.question: Can it diagnose native module problems?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides checks for pods, Gradle, Expo prebuilds, compatibility, and platform logs.

faq.question: What information should I provide first?
faq.answer: Provide the error message, stack trace, platform, build type, package manager, and recent changes.

faq.question: Are any commands risky?
faq.answer: Yes. Some examples delete caches, reinstall dependencies, reset simulators, or kill processes. Confirm the target before running them.

faq.question: Does it replace Flipper or platform IDE tools?
faq.answer: No. It helps decide when and how to use tools such as Flipper, Xcode, Android Studio, and device logs.
